summary: Surveys LLM-based human-agent systems (LLM-HAS) that fold human information, feedback, or control into agent pipelines to improve reliability and safety, organizing core components (profiling, feedback, interaction, orchestration), emerging applications, and open challenges.

Proposes proactive text-to-image agents that ask clarifying questions when uncertain and present an editable belief graph of inferred user intent; human studies and automated evaluation show over 2x higher VQAScore alignment than standard single-turn generation.

Shows that integrating phonemic transcriptions via enhanced in-context-learning retrieval narrows the performance gap between Latin and non-Latin script languages and improves multilingual knowledge transfer in text-based LLMs at inference time.

Introduces oab-bench, 105 Brazilian Bar Exam questions with grading guidelines for evaluating legal writing; Claude-3.5 Sonnet passes all 21 exams (7.93/10 average), and frontier models like o1 correlate strongly with human graders as automated judges.

Introduces Flow-Lenia, a mass-conservative extension of the Lenia continuous cellular automaton that produces stable complex self-organizing patterns, embeds rule parameters within its own dynamics for multispecies simulation, and exhibits emergent evolutionary dynamics.

Compares activation-monitoring techniques for flagging unsafe LM outputs: linear probing, prompted probing, and SAE-based pooling. Zero-shot prompting is a reasonable baseline, but activation and prompted probing substantially outperform it given sufficient training data.

Anthropic Economic Index analysis comparing Claude Code and Claude.ai usage: the coding agent shows 79% task automation versus 49%, web development dominates usage, and startups adopt agentic coding tools far faster than enterprises.

Shows existing vulnerability-detection datasets suffer poor labels, duplication, and leakage, inflating code-LM scores; introduces PrimeVul with better labeling and chronological splits, where a 7B model drops from 68% F1 on BigVul to 3% on PrimeVul.

A theoretical account of when in-context learning emerges versus disappears, via a gating model choosing between in-weight and in-context predictors; generalization and regret analysis identifies the distributional conditions, corroborated on transformers and fine-tuned LLMs.

Converts pretrained LLMs into parameter-sharing Recursive Transformers that loop a single block of layers, adding loop-specific low-rank adapters for layer specialization; with distillation they rival full-size models, plus a Continuous Depth-wise Batching inference scheme.

Position paper introducing 'generative ghosts'—AI agents modeled on specific deceased people that generate novel content—mapping the design space and practical and ethical implications, and proposing a research agenda for safe, beneficial AI afterlives.

Empirical study of how industry practitioners adopt LLMs into data-curation workflows, documenting current practices, use cases, perceived benefits, and challenges of integrating LLMs into data pipelines.

MELODI is a hierarchical memory architecture for long contexts that compresses short-term memory recurrently across layers and consolidates long-term memory in a single middle layer; it matches dense Memorizing Transformers while cutting memory footprint 8x.

Investigates whether finetuning on synthetic documents can insert or modify specific factual beliefs in LLMs, exploring this as a safety tool for building model organisms and reducing risk from advanced AI systems.

Anthropic outlines a research program on model welfare, taking seriously the possibility that future AI systems could warrant moral consideration, and commits to investigating welfare-relevant states and pursuing low-cost precautionary interventions.

Through artistic inquiry and situated dialogue with non-Western art communities, this work reimagines critical AI futures, foregrounding cultural perspectives typically marginalized in dominant AI discourse.

Introduces an anatomically detailed whole-body model of Drosophila in a physics simulator with fluid and adhesion force models; end-to-end RL trains neural controllers for naturalistic walking, flight, and vision-guided hierarchical motor control.

Argues that even without solving alignment, layered safeguards ('bumpers') can reliably catch and correct misalignment during development, outlining how iterative detect-and-fix loops could keep increasingly capable models safe.

A method to infer interparticle interaction potentials directly from observed stochastic trajectories by maximizing trajectory likelihood; it works in and out of equilibrium without assuming a functional form, recovering depletion interactions from colloidal-sphere experiments.

Benchmarks six LLMs on classifying crisis-related social media posts across events, information types, and linguistic features; GPT-4o and GPT-4 generalize best, but all models struggle with flood data, urgent-request categories, and inputs containing typos.

Extends optimal web-refresh crawling to exploit noisy change signals such as sitemap pings; the scalable, decentralized algorithm maintains cache freshness under bandwidth limits with fair per-page performance and a constant total crawl rate.

Argues against the 'Axiom of Rational Convergence,' contending AI safety should ask how diverse communities can coexist rather than converge on universal values; advocates context-appropriate norms and polycentric governance over a single alignment target.

aiXamine is a black-box evaluation platform aggregating 40+ benchmarks across eight safety and security dimensions; testing 50+ LLMs surfaced vulnerabilities including adversarial weakness in GPT-4o, bias in Grok-3, and privacy gaps in Gemini 2.0.
Analyzes 700,000 real-world Claude conversations to build the first large-scale empirical taxonomy of values an AI expresses in practice, examining how expressed values shift across contexts.

Real-world case study showing lightweight prompt-injection attacks—delivered via user input, web retrieval, and system-level agent instructions—can persistently manipulate ChatGPT's behavior and bypass safety filters; framed as a security alert rather than an attack guide.

Uses sparse autoencoders to mechanistically dissect in-context learning in Gemma-1 2B, identifying abstract features that encode and causally trigger the task; scales sparse feature-circuit analysis to link task-detection features to task-execution features via attention and MLPs.

A multi-agent pipeline combining a vision-language model, an LLM, and CLIP for zero-shot detection of novel hazardous objects in driving scenes; extends the COOOL benchmark with natural-language hazard descriptions and evaluates via semantic similarity.

Argues prehospital large-vessel-occlusion detection should not rely on a single sensitivity/specificity threshold; using optical blood-flow imaging, shows thresholds can be titrated to suit the diverse needs of different prehospital systems of care.

Scales the Cell2Sentence framework to a 27B-parameter LLM trained on 1B+ tokens of transcriptomic data, text, and metadata; RL fine-tuning boosts perturbation and reasoning tasks, and a virtual screen nominated silmitasertib for context-selective antigen presentation, validated experimentally.

Examines how companion AI systems affect human relationships, weighing potential risks and benefits and proposing design considerations for building companion AI that supports rather than harms users' social well-being.
